channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: U.S. Secretary of War comments on India-Pakistan understanding and outlines Indo-Pacific security approach
item.description: On May 30, 2026, at the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, U.S. Secretary of War Pete Hegseth praised President Donald Trump for his claimed role in fostering peace between India and Pakistan after las...

item.title: KPMG Australia chief executive resigns amid whistleblower allegations and data leak fallout involving Telstra and Optus
item.description: KPMG Australia has seen its chief executive depart as part of a widening leaks scandal that allegedly involved misuse of confidential client data to win work. Andrew Yates, who had publicly pushed bac...

item.title: Families of slain officers criticise sale of Wieambilla property linked to 2022 police ambush
item.description: The families of two Queensland police officers killed in the 2022 Wieambilla ambush have publicly condemned the sale of the rural property where the attack occurred, describing it as deeply distressin...

item.title: Reform UK criticises Andy Burnham over handling of grooming gang scandal amid by-election campaign
item.description: Reform UK has intensified its criticism of Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham, accusing him of failing victims of the grooming gang scandal. The party’s shadow Home Secretary, Zia Yusuf, made the a...

item.title: Broos backs Lyle Foster after missed penalty in Bafana draw with Nicaragua
item.description: Bafana Bafana head coach Hugo Broos has publicly backed striker Lyle Foster after the forward missed a penalty in South Africa’s goalless draw against Nicaragua in a friendly international played in J...

item.title: Simba Sports Club Publishes Key Details from Its Organizational Charter
item.description: Simba Sports Club from Dar es Salaam has gone public with some important details from their official club charter, making fans and football stakeholders in Tanzania pay close attention. The charter ou...

item.title: James Reid and BINI Receive Most Nominations at the 17th Star Awards for Music
item.description: The 17th Star Awards for Music has announced its official nominations, with James Reid and BINI leading the list this year. The awards, presented by the Philippine Movie Press Club (PMPC), recognize o...

item.title: Long-distance cyclists raise funds and awareness for cancer research in honour of loved ones
item.description: A group of cyclists are taking part in long-distance rides covering hundreds of kilometres to raise money and awareness for cancer research, motivated largely by personal connections to the disease. M...

item.title: James Orengo reports alleged attacks on his convoy during Homa Bay political tour
item.description: Siaya Governor James Orengo has reported a series of alleged violent incidents during his political tour of Homa Bay County, claiming that his convoy was targeted by organised groups of attackers. In ...

item.title: Botswana expands pensions but elder care services lag behind
item.description: Botswana has made notable progress in providing financial support for its older population through the expansion of its old-age pension system. Over the past 20 years, the number of citizens aged 60 a...

item.title: Kwara communities hold Eid prayers at mosques due to security concerns
item.description: For this year Eid-el-Kabir, many communities for Kwara State waka different path as insecurity don force dem to change how dem dey celebrate. Communities like Ijara-Isin, Iwo, Oke-Abba and Owu-Isin no...

item.title: SWS: Sara Duterte records slight increase in net satisfaction rating in March 2026 survey
item.description: Based on a Social Weather Stations (SWS) survey conducted from March 24 to 31, 2026, Vice President Sara Duterte registered a slight improvement in her net satisfaction rating despite ongoing impeachm...

item.title: Deputy Minister Encourages CBE Students to Gain Practical Skills for Career Growth
item.description: On May 29, 2026, the College of Business Education (CBE) hosted its Career Fair under the theme “Innovation and Skills for Youth: The Foundation of Business and Industrial Growth in Tanzania.” Deputy ...

item.title: South Africa’s Reserve Bank signals higher interest rates amid inflation and low growth risks
item.description: The South African Reserve Bank (SARB) has raised the repo rate by 25 basis points to 7%, signalling concerns over a high-inflation, low-growth environment. Governor Lesetja Kganyago outlined three key...

item.title: Authorities raid suspected shabu laboratory on remote island in Occidental Mindoro; two suspects arrested
item.description: Authorities conducted a raid on a remote island in Occidental Mindoro after receiving information about a suspected shabu laboratory operating in the area. Law enforcement officials, including units f...

item.title: White House physician confirms Trump’s overall good health despite minor swelling and bruising
item.description: US President Donald Trump has been declared to be in excellent overall health following a recent medical examination, according to a memo released by the White House. The assessment was conducted duri...

item.title: Experts say Meghan Markle’s use of family imagery supports her lifestyle brand as Lilibet turns five
item.description: The article examines how Meghan Markle’s approach to sharing images of her daughter, Lilibet, has evolved over the past year as the child approaches her fifth birthday. According to the report, fans h...

item.title: Grace Lee Whitney remembered for her role as Yeoman Rand and lifelong advocacy
item.description: Grace Lee Whitney (1930–2015) was an American actress and singer best known for portraying Yeoman Janice Rand on the original Star Trek series (1966–1967). The article highlights her striking appearan...

item.title: Defence Chief clarifies criteria for rehabilitating ex-insurgents under Nigeria’s deradicalisation programme
item.description: Nigeria’s Chief of Defence Staff, Christopher Musa, has clarified the government’s position on the rehabilitation and reintegration of former insurgents into society, stressing that only individuals w...

item.title: Hegseth urges quieter U.S. defense posture in Asia after Trump China visit amid allied security concerns
item.description: Following a recent visit by former President Trump to China, U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has signaled a shift in tone regarding Washington’s approach to Beijing and broader Indo-Pacific defens...
